repo_notes.content: SCOPE & OUTPUT CONTRACT: Generate EXACTLY three English-only pages — 'Skill Inventory', 'Technical Evidence', 'Quantifiables' — plus conditionally 'Dependency Graph' (include ONLY for repos with inter-process communication such as ROS nodes, microservices, or multi-process pipelines; omit for single-process libraries and note omission in Skill Inventory prose). Zero Chinese text. These pages are STRUCTURED RAW DATA for an automated extraction pipeline (Stage 2: Extractor). Do NOT generate resume bullets, accomplishment statements, or text intended for direct resume inclusion. Your job is EVIDENCE EXTRACTION, not resume writing. Every data element must participate in the traceability chain: taxonomy skill (S{nn}) → evidence unit (E{nn}) → quantifiable metric (M{nn}). Do NOT extract data that cannot bind to at least one link in this chain.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: TAXONOMY BINDING: The file .devin/taxonomy.yaml defines 8 tracks (T1-T8) × 5 levels (L0-L4). Each level contains a 'skills' map of snake_case keys, each with an 'aliases' array. This taxonomy is the SOLE AUTHORITY for skill identity. Emit ONLY (taxonomy_ref, skill_name) pairs that exist in the file. Format: taxonomy_ref = 'T{1-8}-L{0-4}', skill_name = snake_case key under that level. The workflow is SKILL-FIRST: iterate each taxonomy skill, search the codebase using its aliases, then record findings. Do NOT scan the codebase first and try to retrofit findings to the taxonomy. Technologies with no taxonomy match go under unmapped_skills with suggested (track, level) and rationale. Levels (L0-L4) serve as CLASSIFICATION tiers for organizing skills by complexity, not as proficiency truth claims.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: YAML FORMAT CONTRACT: Each page MUST contain exactly ONE primary fenced YAML code block (```yaml ... ```) as its machine-readable payload. Supplementary discussion goes OUTSIDE as Markdown prose. Requirements: (1) Parseable by strict YAML 1.2 parser. (2) 2-space indentation, no tabs. (3) Strings containing ':', '#', or leading special chars must be single-quoted. (4) No trailing commas. (5) Block MUST begin with schema_version, source_repo, generated_at, and _meta keys in that order. Stage 2 extracts the FIRST ```yaml``` fenced block per page and parses it — ensure it is self-contained and complete. Prose sections after the YAML block use Markdown ## headings.
repo_notes.author: szzhu
szzhu

repo_notes.content:
ID SCHEMA & CROSS-REFERENCE INTEGRITY: Entity IDs are namespaced by page:
  S{nn} — Skill Inventory entries (S01, S02, ...)
  E{nn} — Technical Evidence units (E01, E02, ...)
  M{nn} — Quantifiable metrics (M01, M02, ...)
  P{nn} — Pipeline chains in Dependency Graph (P01, P02, ...)
Forward refs (authority direction): Skill.evidence_refs → [E{nn}], Skill.metric_refs → [M{nn}], Evidence.metric_refs → [M{nn}].
Reverse refs (validation): Evidence.skill_refs → [S{nn}], Metric.evidence_refs → [E{nn}].
Integrity rules enforced by Stage 2: (1) Every S{nn} must appear in ≥1 E{nn}.skill_refs. (2) Every E{nn} must appear in ≥1 S{nn}.evidence_refs. (3) M{nn} refs are optional — not all evidence produces metrics. (4) No dangling references: every referenced ID must exist in its target page. (5) Integration-category evidence MUST have ≥2 entries in skill_refs. Stage 2 rejects pages with integrity violations and requests re-extraction.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content:
USAGE DEPTH CLASSIFICATION: Each skill receives a usage_depth enum imposing a CEILING on the claimable taxonomy level:
  'declared' → Ceiling L0 (dependency manifests only: package.xml, requirements.txt, find_package).
  'imported' → Ceiling L1 (import/include + default API calls, no customization).
  'configured' → Ceiling L2 (parameters tuned, config/launch files authored for this use case).
  'customized' → Ceiling L3 (source-level modifications — subclassed, extended, custom plugins, non-trivial algorithms).
  'architected' → Ceiling L4 (system designed around this tech — defined interfaces, made trade-offs, wrote abstractions consumed by others).
Ceiling check: parse the level digit from taxonomy_ref and compare against the ceiling. Example: T3-L3 + configured (ceiling L2) → FAIL. Each skill entry must include ceiling_check: 'PASS' or 'FAIL: L{n} exceeds {depth} ceiling L{n}'. When uncertain between two depths, ALWAYS classify DOWN.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content: ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL: (1) Cannot locate a file path → file: '[not found]', classification: 'declared', tag '[inferred]'. Stage 2 will attempt resolution. (2) Line numbers uncertain beyond ±20 → '~' prefix AND confidence: low. (3) Uncertain between usage depths → choose LOWER. (4) NEVER round metrics: extract EXACT values (47.3 stays 47.3, not '~50'). (5) README performance claims without code/test backing → source_type: 'claimed', flag: '[unverified]'. (6) Fewer than 5 extractable metrics is acceptable — do NOT pad with estimates. Quality over quantity. (7) Do NOT infer skill relationships without shared code regions as evidence. (8) Derived values → flag: '[derived]', derivation field MUST show the formula.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

repo_notes.content:
PROVENANCE METADATA: Every page's YAML block MUST begin with these top-level keys in order:
  schema_version: '2.0'
  source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
  generated_at: '<ISO-8601 timestamp>'
  _meta:
    files_scanned: <int>
    files_in_repo: <int>
    scan_coverage_pct: <float 0-100>
    directories_skipped: ['<path>']
    extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
    confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
    known_gaps: ['<description>']
Stage 2 triggers re-extraction when: scan_coverage_pct < 60 OR extraction_confidence = 'low' OR len(known_gaps) > 3.
repo_notes.author: szzhu

pages.title: Skill Inventory
pages.purpose: For each (taxonomy_ref, skill_name) pair in .devin/taxonomy.yaml, search the codebase for evidence using its aliases. Classify usage depth with ceiling checks, extract ATS keywords, and document file-level proficiency evidence. Partition into matched_skills (taxonomy-bound) and unmapped_skills (observed but absent from taxonomy). Include tech_stack summary. This page ANCHORS the traceability chain — every downstream evidence unit and metric must link back to at least one skill entry here.

page_notes.content:
YAML SCHEMA — Skill Inventory:
```yaml
schema_version: '2.0'
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
generated_at: '<ISO-8601>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>']
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']

matched_skills:
  - id: 'S{nn}'
    taxonomy_ref: 'T{n}-L{n}'
    skill_name: '<snake_case key from taxonomy>'
    usage_depth: <declared|imported|configured|customized|architected>
    ceiling_check: '<PASS | FAIL: L{n} exceeds {depth} ceiling L{n}>'
    evidence_summary: '<≤30 words describing codebase usage of this skill>'
    file_count: <int>
    proficiency_evidence:
      - file: '<path>'
        lines: '<start-end>'
        classification: <implemented|configured|declared|tested>
        summary: '<≤20 words>'
        confidence: <high|medium|low>
    ats_keywords:
      exact: ['<verbatim from taxonomy aliases>']
      semantic:
        - keyword: '<code-observed term not in aliases>'
          source_file: '<path where observed>'
    evidence_refs: ['E{nn}']
    metric_refs: ['M{nn}']

unmapped_skills:
  - name: '<technology name>'
    observed_in: '<file path>'
    usage_depth: <declared|imported|configured|customized|architected>
    suggested_track: 'T{n}'
    suggested_level: 'L{n}'
    rationale: '<why this track and level>'

tech_stack:
  languages:
    - name: '<language>'
      version: '<if detectable>'
      loc_pct: <float>
  frameworks:
    - name: '<framework>'
      version: '<if detectable>'
      role: '<middleware|web|testing|build|etc>'
  build_tools:
    - name: '<tool>'
      version: '<if detectable>'
  testing:
    - framework: '<name>'
      test_count_est: <int>
```
After the YAML block, include Markdown prose sections:
## Architecture Context
2-3 paragraphs on how components compose into a working system. Identify major subsystems and their responsibilities.
## Skill Relationship Overview
Describe which skills co-occur in the same code regions, what integration patterns exist, and which files demonstrate the coupling. Reference integration evidence unit IDs (E{nn}) from the Technical Evidence page. This section is the PRIMARY guide for Stage 3 when composing cross-track resume bullets.
If this is a single-process library with no IPC, append: 'Dependency Graph page omitted: single-process library with no IPC architecture.'

ATS KEYWORD RULES: The 'aliases' arrays in taxonomy.yaml are the PRIMARY ATS keyword source. Partition into 'exact' (drawn VERBATIM from aliases, case-preserved) and 'semantic' (additional terms observed in code — class names, function names, API calls, topic names, message types — that are NOT already in the aliases array). Do NOT duplicate aliases into semantic. Each semantic keyword must cite its source_file.

RELATIONSHIP DISCOVERY: Skill Inventory does NOT carry a formal relationship graph — integration structure lives in Technical Evidence as integration-category evidence units (category: integration, ≥2 skill_refs). However, the '## Skill Relationship Overview' prose MUST describe observed co-occurrences and name the relevant E{nn} IDs. Two skills are related ONLY if they interact in the same file or call chain — co-existing in the same repo without code-level interaction is NOT a relationship.

pages.title: Technical Evidence
pages.purpose: Extract atomic evidence units — factual statements about what the code does, how, and why — each with file-level citations. Categories: 'implementation' (algorithms, API usage, data structures), 'configuration' (parameter and launch choices), 'integration' (cross-component interaction, MUST reference ≥2 skills), 'architecture' (system-design decisions), 'testing' (test coverage and approach). Also extract project_context. Do NOT write resume bullets or accomplishment claims — extract FACTS that Stage 3 composes into bullets.

page_notes.content:
YAML SCHEMA — Technical Evidence:
```yaml
schema_version: '2.0'
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
generated_at: '<ISO-8601>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>']
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']

project_context:
  domain: '<problem domain, e.g. autonomous navigation, web API, data pipeline>'
  purpose: '<1-2 sentences: what this project does>'
  deployment_context: '<embedded|cloud|local|simulation|other>'

evidence_units:
  - id: 'E{nn}'
    category: <implementation|configuration|integration|architecture|testing>
    skill_refs: ['S{nn}']
    summary: '<≤50 words: neutral factual statement of what the code does>'
    detail: '<≤150 words: deeper technical specifics>'
    citation:
      file: '<path>'
      lines: '<start-end>'
      confidence: <high|medium|low>
    classification: <implemented|configured|declared|tested>
    keywords: ['<technical terms from this evidence>']
    metric_refs: ['M{nn}']
    integration_detail: null
```
For integration-category evidence units, replace integration_detail null with:
```yaml
    integration_detail:
      relationship_type: <co_implementation|pipeline_stage|dependency|interface_boundary|runtime_composition>
      pattern: '<≤30 words: how the referenced skills interact architecturally>'
      co_occurrence_files: <int>
```
Relationship types map to Stage 3 bullet clause patterns:
  co_implementation → 'Implemented X with Y'
  pipeline_stage → 'X feeding into Y'
  dependency → 'X built on Y'
  interface_boundary → 'X communicating with Y via Z'
  runtime_composition → 'X orchestrating Y at runtime'

EVIDENCE QUALITY RULES: (1) Every evidence unit must cite ≥1 source file with line range. (2) 'classification' determines evidence strength: 'implemented' and 'configured' = proficiency evidence; 'tested' = supporting; 'declared' = weak and flagged for review. (3) 'summary' MUST be a neutral factual statement, NOT an accomplishment claim. BAD: 'Achieved 50% latency reduction'. GOOD: 'Implements A* with pruning heuristic; config sets planning loop to 100Hz'. (4) integration-category units are the PRIMARY input for cross-track resume bullets in Stage 3 — clearly document which skills interact and HOW via integration_detail. (5) 'detail' may include design rationale evident from code comments or structure, but must remain factual. (6) 'keywords' capture technical terms beyond taxonomy aliases — these feed ATS keyword optimization in Stage 3.

pages.title: Quantifiables
pages.purpose: Extract ALL numbers, metrics, and quantifiable signals from the codebase. Source types: 'configured' (parameters, gains, rates, tolerances), 'counted' (files, functions, test cases, LOC per module), 'measured' (benchmarks, test results, profiled values), 'claimed' (README/docs assertions — always flag '[unverified]'). Extract raw values with units and source citations. Do NOT interpret or spin these numbers — Stage 3 decides which become resume metrics.

page_notes.content:
YAML SCHEMA — Quantifiables:
```yaml
schema_version: '2.0'
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
generated_at: '<ISO-8601>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>']
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']

metrics:
  - id: 'M{nn}'
    name: '<snake_case descriptive name>'
    value: <exact number>
    unit: '<unit string>'
    source_type: <measured|configured|counted|claimed>
    citation:
      file: '<path>'
      lines: '<start-end>'
      confidence: <high|medium|low>
    evidence_refs: ['E{nn}']
    flag: null
    derivation: null
    context: '<≤20 words: what this number represents in the system>'
```
When flag is not null, allowed values: '[unverified]' (claimed with no code backing), '[derived]' (calculated — derivation field MUST show formula, e.g. '45 tests × 3 suites = 135 total'), '[est.]' (estimated — derivation field MUST show rationale).

EXTRACTION PRIORITY: (1) Configured parameters — control gains, frequencies, buffer sizes, tolerances, timeouts. (2) Code-structure counts — source files, functions/methods, test cases, modules, LOC per component. (3) Performance values — loop rates, latencies, queue depths, message sizes. (4) Test and benchmark results — pass rates, coverage percentages, timing results. (5) README/doc claims — only if no code evidence exists for the claim; always tag '[unverified]'.

RULES: (a) Always include raw value + unit + source file citation. (b) If a number appears in multiple places with different values, extract ALL occurrences as separate metrics and note the discrepancy in context. (c) Derived metrics require trivially verifiable calculations — always show formula in derivation. (d) Percentages must note the denominator in context. (e) If the repo yields < 5 metrics, that is acceptable — do NOT pad with estimates.

pages.title: Dependency Graph
pages.purpose: Extract the runtime communication architecture. For ROS/ROS2: nodes, topics (with message types and QoS), services, actions, TF frames, launch composition. For non-ROS: inter-process communication, API boundaries, event buses, message queues, shared-memory interfaces, plugin architectures. Key output: pipeline_chains (P{nn}) — end-to-end data flow paths that Stage 3 uses for system-level resume bullets. CONDITIONAL: If the repo is a single-process library with NO IPC, SKIP this page entirely and note in Skill Inventory prose: 'Dependency Graph omitted: single-process library with no IPC architecture.'

page_notes.content:
YAML SCHEMA — Dependency Graph:
```yaml
schema_version: '2.0'
source_repo: '<owner/repo>'
generated_at: '<ISO-8601>'
_meta:
  files_scanned: <int>
  files_in_repo: <int>
  scan_coverage_pct: <float>
  directories_skipped: ['<path>']
  extraction_confidence: <high|medium|low>
  confidence_rationale: '<1 sentence>'
  known_gaps: ['<description>']

architecture_type: <ros2|ros1|microservice|monolith|plugin_based|hybrid>

components:
  - name: '<component or node name>'
    source_file: '<path>'
    type: <node|service|process|container|plugin>
    lifecycle_managed: <true|false|null>
    interfaces:
      publishes:
        - channel: '<topic or endpoint>'
          data_type: '<message or data type>'
          frequency_hz: <number|null>
          qos_profile: '<if specified|null>'
          citation: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>', confidence: <high|medium|low>}
      subscribes:
        - channel: '<topic or endpoint>'
          data_type: '<message or data type>'
          callback_citation: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>', confidence: <high|medium|low>}
      serves:
        - name: '<service or endpoint name>'
          interface_type: '<service or API type>'
          role: <server|client>
          citation: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>', confidence: <high|medium|low>}
      actions:
        - name: '<action name>'
          action_type: '<action type>'
          role: <server|client>
          supports_preemption: <true|false|null>
          citation: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>', confidence: <high|medium|low>}
    parameters:
      - name: '<param name>'
        type: '<data type>'
        default: '<value>'
        dynamic: <true|false>
        citation: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>', confidence: <high|medium|low>}

tf_tree:
  - parent: '<frame>'
    child: '<frame>'
    publisher: '<component name>'
    static: <true|false>

launch_composition:
  - launch_file: '<path>'
    components_launched: ['<name>']
    includes: ['<other launch file>']
    conditionals: ['<brief description of conditional logic>']

pipeline_chains:
  - id: 'P{nn}'
    name: '<descriptive name, e.g. perception_to_control>'
    chain: '<component→channel→component→channel→component>'
    latency_budget_ms: <number|null>
    latency_source: {file: '<path>', lines: '<range>', confidence: <high|medium|low>}
    skill_refs: ['S{nn}']
    evidence_refs: ['E{nn}']
```
After the YAML block, include:
## System Architecture Narrative
2-3 paragraphs describing overall data flow from sensor inputs to actuator outputs (or request to response, etc.).
## Concurrency & Real-Time Design
Threading model, executor type, callback groups, priority settings — with file citations. For non-ROS repos, describe the concurrency model (thread pools, async/await, event loops) with equivalent detail.

PIPELINE CHAIN RULES: pipeline_chains are the highest-value output of this page. Stage 3 uses them to compose system-level resume bullets demonstrating architectural ownership. Requirements: (1) Each chain names all components and channels in sequence. (2) skill_refs lists ALL taxonomy skills involved in the chain. (3) evidence_refs points to integration-category evidence units in Technical Evidence that describe the same data flow. (4) For non-ROS repos, adapt chains to the relevant IPC mechanism: HTTP call chains, message queue flows, shared-memory pipelines, event bus sequences. (5) Include latency_budget_ms with citation if the codebase specifies timing constraints.

Relationship to other pages: pipeline_chains.skill_refs should be a SUPERSET of the skill_refs from integration evidence units that describe the same data flow. This enables Stage 3 to generate cross-track bullets like 'Designed a perception-to-control pipeline spanning [list of skills from chain.skill_refs]'. The tf_tree section is ROS-specific — omit entirely for non-ROS repos. launch_composition likewise applies only to systems with declarative launch/orchestration files.
